scoring_methodology: STS-primary for all elements (consistent with STRM-001–004). Competency_implication field bridges vocabulary gap between legal prose and competency language.

mappings.fde_description: Knowledge of AI literacy program design, including assessment of staff technical knowledge, experience, education and training needs, contextual adaptation of AI literacy content to operational contexts, and consideration of affected populations in AI literacy planning.
mappings.ref_statement: Knowledge of AI literacy concepts, including what AI is and is not, how ML models work at a conceptual level, AI limitations and failure modes, and responsible AI principles for non-technical audiences.
mappings.notes: EU AI Act Art. 4 (competency) superset of WIDAI AG domain. AG-K-009 is the strongest match (STS: 0.590, cosine: 0.723). Secondary: OP-T-006 (OP, cosine: 0.707), OP-T-004 (OP, cosine: 0.576).

mappings.fde_description: Knowledge of AI risk management system design, including establishment, implementation, documentation, and maintenance of risk management processes specific to AI systems throughout the system lifecycle.
mappings.ref_statement: Knowledge of AI governance structures, including model risk management, AI lifecycle oversight, accountability assignment, and the organizational conditions that enable responsible AI deployment at scale.
mappings.notes: EU AI Act Art. 9(1) (obligation) equal WIDAI AG domain. AG-K-002 is the strongest match (STS: 0.679, cosine: 0.734). Secondary: AG-K-003 (AG, cosine: 0.725), RC-T-012 (RC, cosine: 0.705).

mappings.fde_description: Skill in conducting iterative AI risk assessment across the system lifecycle, including risk identification and analysis, risk estimation under intended use and foreseeable misuse conditions, integration of post-market monitoring data into risk evaluation, and design of targeted risk management measures.
mappings.ref_statement: Skill in designing risk appetite statements for data and AI activities that are specific enough to guide operational risk decisions yet flexible enough to accommodate business evolution and emerging use cases.

mappings.fde_description: Ability to assess interaction effects between multiple AI system requirements and design balanced risk management measures that address combined risks while maintaining regulatory compliance across all applicable requirements.
